channel.title: Data-Parallelism on Cedric Bail
channel.description: Recent content in Data-Parallelism on Cedric Bail

item.title: Data Parallelism: simpler solution for Golang?
item.description:
Warning

Historical note. This post predates the actual TinyGo SPMD compiler. It’s a thought experiment from when the design space was still open. The high-level ideas (uniform vs varying, go for as the SPMD construct, masking for divergent control flow) all survived, but some details have evolved: lanes.Varying[T] is now a package-typed magic generic (no varying keyword), and several restrictions on return/break, function visibility, and go for nesting were added during implementation. For accurate, measured behavior see Writing SPMD Go and SPMD Results.
